mnemonics: SEQUENCE TEXT RC: Sequence Text for Row-Column. Helps remember the three main formats in order of performance/use case complexity.
keyPoints: SEQUENCEFILE: The original Hive format, good for storing binary data and splittable for parallel processing.
TEXTFILE: The default, human-readable format (like CSV). Simple but inefficient for querying.
RCFILE (Record Columnar File): A row-columnar hybrid format that offers better compression and query performance for column-based operations.
commonMistakes: Using TEXTFILE for large-scale analytical queries, which leads to very poor performance due to lack of compression and the need to parse all fields.
Assuming all file formats support splittability and compression equally, which can lead to inefficient storage and processing if not chosen correctly for the workload.
